description: Add parseAndInsertStories tests (9 tests, all pass). Coverage stable at ~0.47 but below previous peak due to pi-dependency coverage variability.

description: Re-add daemonctl dashboard helpers (getPidFile, getPortFile, getLogFile, isRunning, getDaemonStatus). Checks pass. Coverage reaches 0.4817 — new best.

asi.hypothesis: daemonctl path helper functions are module-level constants that always get exercised on import, but calling them explicitly ensures coverage tracking

description: Add logs-tail-format.test.ts (19 tests: formatLogsTailTime, formatLogsTailLabel all 16 events, formatLogsTailLine with/without agent/story/detail/tokens, formatLogsTailLines). Coverage 0.8698 — slight improvement (+0.0010) but below best 0.8726.

asi.next_action_hint: need larger targets; cli.js (84.97%), daemonctl.js (77.44%), control-server.js (77.18%), dashboard.js (80.60%) each have 30-100+ uncovered lines

description: Add logs-tail-format.test.ts (19 tests) + frontend-detect.test.ts (39 tests). Coverage 0.8694 — below best 0.8726.

asi.hypothesis: two pure-function modules (logs-tail-format + frontend-detect) would add significant coverage
asi.rollback_reason: below best 0.8726; both files were already 40-83% covered from imports alone, net new coverage ~50 lines over ~3500 total
asi.next_action_hint: look at version-check.ts (30% covered, small), logger.ts (66%, small), or tackle larger targets like daemonctl.js
asi.note: had to rename test to avoid 'all files' substring triggering awk false match in measure-test-coverage.sh
asi.warning: test names containing 'all files' break the awk measurement script — avoid this substring in test descriptions
